scope.summary: Add a UV coverage completeness check asserting all panels including back, side, sleeve, and lining panels receive a UV island in the packed atlas, and surface a blocking validation failure when any panel has no UV island
acceptance_criteria: pack_uv_islands processes every PanelSpec in the GarmentSpec regardless of panel_id naming convention or panel orientation (front, back, side, sleeve, lining)
UV_COVERAGE ValidationDescriptor returns Blocking failure when any panel_id present in tailor_garments.spec_json is absent from tailor_uv_islands for the same (garment_id, simulation_run_id) pair
The TailorUvIslandsPacked event payload includes a panel_coverage_ratio field (islands_packed / total_panels) so models can detect partial coverage without querying the database
An integration test drapes a 6-panel garment (front, back, left-side, right-side, left-sleeve, right-sleeve) and asserts all 6 panels have UV island rows in tailor_uv_islands
proof_targets: HBR-INT: UV_COVERAGE check runs in validation catalog and blocks promotion when a back panel UV island is absent (MT-153 cross-check)
scope.risk_if_missed: Garments with back or side panels missing UV islands export with holes in the texture atlas. Downstream DCC tools show black or default-material panels on the garment back. The issue may not surface until Blender import review, causing rework.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
